extraction.one_line_summary: PEDERSEN Mads won from peloton sprint after long lead-out by STEUYVEN Jasper, sustaining power through rising uphill finish.
tactical_patterns: STEUYVEN led out PEDERSEN from 1km+ despite downhill-to-uphill transition, unusual distance for modern sprint trains.
Trek successfully isolated their sprint train without full team control at front, suggesting numerical superiority less critical on gradient finishes.
YATES Simon lost 47 seconds in late crash, demonstrating GC vulnerability on supposedly safe sprint stages.

rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: holds power on rising finishes better than pure sprinters, suggesting target uphill sprint stages specifically
rider_signals.STEUYVEN Jasper: positive: demonstrated diesel engine capacity to lead out from abnormally long distance on gradient finish
rider_signals.PHILLIPSEN Jasper: neutral: second place but faded in final meters when gradient increased, confirming flat-finish preference
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: lost 47 seconds to crash positioning on sprint stage, revealing weak team support for late-stage positioning
